summary: One year ago today, on March 29, 2025, the world was reflecting on the fourth anniversary of the Ever Given's dislodgement from the Suez Canal, a pivotal event that had once disrupted global trade. In the United States, news briefings were dominated by ongoing political developments and economic updates, as the nation continued to navigate the complexities of a post-pandemic world. Across Europe, news bulletins provided insights into the continent's response to climate change and the evolving geopolitical landscape. Meanwhile, podcasts and news channels offered in-depth analyses of current events, highlighting the interconnectedness of global affairs. This day served as a reminder of the ever-changing nature of world events and the importance of staying informed.
